entities.description: bookindex is a system or component that serves as the foundation for the described agent based retrieval approach
information foraging theory is the theoretical framework grounding the retrieval process described in the text
selector is a component used to narrow down the search space via information scents
reasoner is a component used to locate highly relevant evidence
user queries are the input items that the agent classifies based on intent and complexity
retrieval workflows are the static processes being addressed and dynamically generated by the agent
the search space is the area narrowed down by the selector component
information scents are the signals used by the selector to narrow down the search space
evidence refers to the highly relevant information located by the reasoner

relations.description: the system builds upon bookindex to implement an agent that uses selector for retrieval workflows
the system builds upon bookindex to implement an agent that uses reasoner for retrieval workflows
the retrieval process using selector is grounded in information foraging theory
the retrieval process using reasoner is grounded in information foraging theory
selector and reasoner work together within the agent based retrieval process to narrow search space and locate evidence
user queries are classified to dynamically generate tailored retrieval workflows
the agent classifies user queries based on their intent and complexity
the agent dynamically generates tailored retrieval workflows
the selector narrows down the search space
the selector uses information scents to narrow down the search space
the reasoner locates highly relevant evidence
the retrieval process mimics foraging as described by information foraging theory
